key: listening13_directions
text: I want to visit the Metropolitan Museum. I stop a friendly man on the street. Excuse me, where is the museum? I ask. It is not far, he says. Go straight for two blocks, then turn left at the corner. The museum is on your right, next to a big park. It is across from the bank. How do I get there? Can I walk? Yes, you can walk, he says. It is about five minutes. I say thank you and I find the museum easily.

key: listening13_lost
text: Yesterday I was a little lost in the city. I could not find the train station. So I asked a woman, how do I get to the station? She said, go straight, turn right at the second corner, and the station is between a hotel and a coffee shop. I followed the directions. Go straight, turn right, and there it was. Asking for directions is easy and people like to help.
